titleStmt.title:
The Consul General at Batavia ( Foote ) to the Secretary of State
Repeated by the Department as telegram 314, May 22, 7 p.m., to The Hague.

div.head:
The Consul General at Batavia ( Foote ) to the Secretary of State
Repeated by the Department as telegram 314, May 22, 7 p.m., to The Hague.
div.p:
210. Van Mook met Sjahrir late yesterday and gave him protocol containing Netherlands’ final word of offer. I have not seen the document but know that one item is to effect Soekarno is unacceptable to Netherlands Government in every way. Hope obtain copy of document near future.The document was summarized in telegram 227, May 27, 10 p.m., from Batavia; it was dated The Hague, April 30. Mr. Foote added: “Van Mook told me he informed Sjahrir verbally Soekarno unacceptable to Netherlands Government and that the above is Netherlands Government’s final offer.” In telegram 226, May 27, 6 p.m., from Batavia, Mr. Foote stated: “Present Dutch offer differs in no important respects from previous offers.” (856E.00/5–2746) The text of the protocol was transmitted in despatch 51, May 30, from Batavia. Sjahrir in depressed mood, said he would proceed
Djokjakarta today or tomorrow. He told Van Mook he might have to resign. If he does resign, such would leave rabid extremists in charge Indonesian movement and probably end all hopes of peaceful agreement.
